meaning: n.小把,小束,小捆,小物体,纤细的东西vt.把...卷成一捆
examples: All we have left is paroxysm, and when it subsides, there will be just one wisp of smoke. Our inner fire will ravish all.
She's just a little bitty wisp of a girl.
She smoothed away a wisp of hair from her eyes.
At first glance, Hagelin seems a wisp, hardly built for the rigors of an NHL campaign.
The lists clung on thanks to a dusty wisp of tape or a lick of long-dried glue.
